red_state.expected: Focused desktop sessions API and workflow component tests compile and run. API type/client dependency contract tests pass because desktop types already expose the fields, while import/export dialog diagnostics tests fail for missing visible dependency diagnostics UI.
failing_tests: WorkflowTemplateManager > renders missing recommended skill diagnostics as import warnings without blocking selection
WorkflowTemplateManager > renders invalid dependency diagnostics as import errors and keeps the candidate unselectable
WorkflowTemplateManager > renders export dependency manifest diagnostics before users reuse the exported JSON
passing_task_cases: sessionsApi workflow contract > exposes import preview dependency diagnostics from the desktop client response type
sessionsApi workflow contract > exposes export dependency manifests from the desktop client response type without skill contents

validation_output.command: cd desktop && bun run test -- --run src/api/sessions.test.ts src/components/workflow/WorkflowComponents.test.tsx
validation_output.summary: src/api/sessions.test.ts: 20 tests passed. src/components/workflow/WorkflowComponents.test.tsx: 38 passed, 3 failed, 41 tests total. Failures are expected RED assertions: import candidates do not render dependencyDiagnostics as visible warning/error diagnostics, and export dialog does not render dependencyManifest diagnostics before reuse; the export JSON textarea contains the manifest but no visible diagnostics section.

concerns: Desktop API types already include WorkflowImportDependencyDiagnostic and WorkflowSkillDependencyManifest fields, so the API contract tests are green rather than RED.
The component test file had pre-existing unrelated edits in the same allowed file; they were left in place and not reverted.
The current export dialog displays dependencyManifest only inside the JSON textarea, which is not sufficient for the requested pre-export/reuse diagnostics visibility.
recovery_hints: In WorkflowImportExportDialog ImportPreview, render candidate.dependencyDiagnostics in a dedicated visible diagnostics section with severity, status, phaseId, reference name/source/plugin, message, and canImport state.

Keep missing recommended skill diagnostics selectable by default when canImport is true; keep invalid-reference diagnostics visually error-toned and ensure canImport false candidates remain unselectable.
After exportWorkflowTemplates returns, render response.dependencyManifest.dependencies as a visible export dependency diagnostics section before or adjacent to the export JSON textarea.

Use wording that frames plugin-disabled, missing, and unsupported-source entries as recommended references only. Do not imply bundled skill contents, auto-execution, default gates, or plugin-primary binding.
Rerun the focused desktop command after implementing the dialog UI.
